summary: Adjusted `scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` so it re-enters the repo `nix develop` shell when host `rustc` is unavailable and no explicit `RUSTC` override was provided. Added a focused regression script, `scripts/test-native-runtime-bootstrap.sh`, that stubs `nix` and verifies the bootstrap path without running the full native runtime harness.
residual_risks: I did not run `bash scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` end-to-end against the real runtime toolchain in this turn.
I did not run `bash scripts/verify-all.sh` per task instruction, so repo-wide verification remains for the verifier.
The checkout already had unrelated uncommitted changes; I left them untouched.

feedback.summary: The prior verifier failure was caused by `scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` assuming a host `rustc`. This patch makes that script align with the repo's existing Nix-based verification model and adds a narrow regression test for the bootstrap branch.
ergonomics: The new bootstrap path only triggers when `rustc` is absent, `RUSTC` was not explicitly set, and the script is not already inside a Nix shell.
An explicit `RUSTC` override still wins, which avoids masking deliberate toolchain configuration during debugging.
follow_ups: Once the verifier is ready, rerun the real `bash scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` in the intended environment.
After that, rerun the repo-wide gate to confirm this unblocks the original verifier failure.
warnings: `scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` already had in-progress user changes in this checkout; I preserved them and layered the Nix bootstrap fix on top.
